reference: research introduces a novel Activity Recom- mendation System built on the COBIT 2019 framework tailored for Financial Balance Scorecard. The motivation stems from the challenges faced by auditors when identifying essential activities aligned with strategy of a company, often hindered by manual COBIT 2019 guideline navigation. To address this, a system was developed, where inputting the company strategy, triggers the display of recommended activities automatically. The methodology involved creating interconnected nodes from COBIT 2019 Element in Neo4J. This research uses a real strategy plan case study from 3 different companies as input, followed by a preprocessing step, then compares it to Enterprise Goals using TF-IDF and cosine similarity. Goals with high similarity scores trigger a graph query to reveal connected

reference: . There are two datasets: unfiltered and filtered. The unfiltered dataset is a collection of log messages in its original condition gathered from the two sources without performing unique filtering. Therefore, there are several duplicate log messages both in training and testing sets. This is aligned with the real case scenario, where a drone tends to generate the same log message when the event is still happening. It indicates that the current event has yet to be changed or solved (if it is an issue). On the other hand, the filtered dataset is a collection of log messages after a unique filtering process. user_input: PREVIOUS WORKS Among the relevant published studies in the log-based anomaly detection space, sequence-based is one of the most commonly used approaches. Therefore, there are limited relevant references as this study employs a point-based approach. Below are the relevant baselines from previous research: 1) Pylogsentiment is the first study which employs sentiment analysis-based anomaly detection on operat- ing system logs using GRU and GloVe embedding. 2) SentiLog , similar to , use a two-layered BiLSTM and GloVe embedding model to perform anomaly detection on parallel file system logs. 3) TransSentLog is a further development of and uses a two-layered transformer encoder which used two attention heads and GloVe embedding along with integrated gradients to add explainability to the trained model.

Drone logs typically include information such as flight paths, altitude, speed, battery life, sensor data, and other telemetry information . Drone logs typically include information such as flight paths, altitude, speed, battery life, sensor data, and other telemetry information . CONCLUSION This research demonstrates that the YOLOv8 model, specifically the YOLOv8m variant, provides excellent results in detecting Peritoneal Carcinomatosis (PC) from the used laparoscopic image dataset. By utilizing and fine-tuning the pre-trained YOLOv8 weights, this model shows superior performance with a mean Average Precision (mAP50) of 0.871, precision of 0.893, recall of 0.785, and an F1 score of 0.84. The predicted images also show that the performance of YOLOv8 with pre-trained weights is able to predict images well. All YOLOv8 scales are accurate in making predictions, with no errors in image prediction. In contrast, without using pre-trained weights, YOLOv8n and YOLOv8s still make errors in prediction, with some lesions being missed.
